entries.title: 1.5L Petrol
entries.history: The workhorse 1.5L VVT-i petrol engine powering most UK grey import Spade models, valued for simplicity and parts sharing with Vitz/Porte.
compatibleVariants: Spade F, Spade L, Spade S, Spade F CVT, Spade L CVT, Spade S CVT, Porte-compatible variants
commonFailures: VVT-i solenoid failure causing rough idle and P0011 codes (60k-90k miles)
Oil consumption due to piston ring wear on high-mileage units (100k+ miles)
CVT transmission compatibility issues if not calibrated post-install

commonFailures: Hybrid inverter failure requiring specialist calibration and HV system diagnostics

Hybrid battery degradation impacting electric-only operation and fuel economy

commonProblems.h2: Toyota Spade Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Toyota Spade's 1.5L 1NZ-FE petrol and 1NZ-FXE hybrid engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, with VVT-i solenoid faults, oil consumption on high-mileage units, and CVT compatibility concerns driving most searches. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers the best value for your grey import Spade.

problems.h4: VVT-i Solenoid Failure - Why Toyota Spade 1NZ-FE Engines Develop Timing Faults
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Spade F, L, S (2012-2020, 1NZ-FE petrol)
problems.rootCause: Oil control valve (part 15330-21010) clogging or electrical failure disrupts variable valve timing, triggering P0011/P0012 codes and rough running.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace oil control valve, reset ECU adaptations, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early; may recur if oil consumption present

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Solenoid + cam phaser inspection, timing chain tensioner check, oil system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses related wear; suitable for moderate-mileage Spade

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt unit with upgraded VVT-i components, bench tested, TCU adaptation included
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution; ideal for high-mileage or fleet Spade owners

problems.recommendation: On a Toyota Spade worth £3,000-£6,000, a standalone solenoid fix offers best value if the engine is otherwise healthy. For units over 100k miles or showing oil consumption, a rebuilt engine with upgraded VVT-i components prevents repeat failures and includes warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 1NZ-FE engine prices for your Toyota Spade

problems.h4: Oil Consumption Fix - Why High-Mileage Toyota Spade Engines Burn Oil
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Spade F, L, S, CVT variants (2012-2020, 1NZ-FE petrol)
problems.rootCause: Piston ring wear and PCV system degradation allow oil into combustion chambers, causing blue smoke, fouled plugs, and catalytic converter damage if ignored.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - PCV Valve + Oil System Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace PCV valve, flush crankcase, use high-mileage oil specification
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary mitigation; does not address ring wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine strip, ring replacement, bore honing, reassembly with upgraded components
repairOptions.longevity: Significant labour cost; viable only for low-mileage, well-maintained Spade

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt 1NZ-FE with Ring Upgrade
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt unit with upgraded piston rings, PCV system, bench dyno tested
repairOptions.longevity: Most cost-effective permanent solution; includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: Piston ring replacement labour often exceeds the value of a grey import Spade. A rebuilt engine with upgraded rings and PCV components delivers better long-term value, eliminates blue smoke, and protects the catalytic converter - all covered by warranty.
problems.cta: Get quotes for oil-consumption-fixed Toyota Spade engines

problems.h4: CVT Transmission Shudder - Why Toyota Spade Super CVT-i Units Develop Drivability Issues
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Spade F CVT, L CVT, S CVT, Hybrid (2012-2020, 1NZ-FE/1NZ-FXE with CVT)
problems.rootCause: CVT fluid degradation, torque converter wear, or incorrect TCU adaptation after engine replacement causes judder, slip, and hesitation under acceleration.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - CVT Fluid Service + Adaptation Reset
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drain/refill with Toyota CVT Fluid FE, reset TCU adaptations, road test calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for early-stage shudder; requires specialist diagnostic equipment

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove transmission, replace torque converter, refill, re-adapt TCU
repairOptions.longevity: Significant labour; only recommended if converter diagnosed as faulty

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with CVT Match
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with verified flex plate, TCU adaptation reset included, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Ensures perfect engine-CVT integration; eliminates adaptation guesswork

problems.recommendation: CVT shudder is often misdiagnosed as an engine fault. If your Spade has drivability issues post-engine-fit, insist on TCU adaptation reset and flex plate verification. A rebuilt engine supplied with CVT compatibility assurance prevents costly post-install diagnostics.
problems.cta: Compare CVT-compatible Toyota Spade engine prices

problems.h4: Hybrid Inverter Fault - Why Toyota Spade 1NZ-FXE Systems Trigger Warning Lights
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Spade Hybrid, ULEZ Hybrid (2016-2020, 1NZ-FXE hybrid)
problems.rootCause: Inverter coolant pump failure, MG1/MG2 sensor degradation, or HV ECU communication errors trigger hybrid system warnings and reduce electric assist performance.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace coolant pump, flush hybrid cooling circuit, reset HV ECU codes
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if pump is sole fault; requires hybrid-certified technician

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic scan, sensor replacement, hybrid system validation test
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses specific sensor faults; not viable for inverter core failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt hybrid engine with inverter calibration, MG sync, 4-hour HV bench test
repairOptions.longevity: Only solution for core inverter failure; includes hybrid specialist warranty

problems.recommendation: Hybrid inverter faults require specialist diagnostics. If your Spade Hybrid shows persistent warning lights after basic repairs, a rebuilt 1NZ-FXE unit with pre-calibrated inverter and MG sync delivers ULEZ compliance certainty and avoids repeated garage visits.
problems.cta: Enquire about rebuilt Toyota Spade hybrid engine availability

problems.h4: Water Pump Failure - Why Toyota Spade 1NZ-FE Engines Overheat
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Spade F, L, S, CVT variants (2012-2020, 1NZ-FE petrol)
problems.rootCause: Water pump bearing wear or impeller degradation causes coolant leaks, overheating, and potential head gasket damage if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace water pump, thermostat, coolant flush, pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: Standard preventative maintenance; viable at first sign of leak

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Pump + thermostat + hoses + radiator inspection, full system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses related wear; suitable for Spade with cooling concerns

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Cooling Upgrade
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt unit with upgraded water pump, thermostat, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents repeat overheating; ideal if head gasket already compromised

problems.recommendation: Catch water pump leaks early. If overheating has occurred, inspect for head gasket damage before committing to pump replacement alone. A rebuilt engine with upgraded cooling components offers peace of mind for high-mileage Spade owners.
problems.cta: Get quotes for overheating-prevented Toyota Spade engines

problems.h4: EGR Valve Clogging - Why Toyota Spade Engines Lose Power and Idle Roughly
problems.affectedModels: Toyota Spade F, L, S (2012-2020, 1NZ-FE petrol)
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup in EGR valve restricts exhaust gas recirculation, causing rough idle, power loss, and failed MOT emissions tests if not addressed.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, clean or replace EGR valve, reset ECU adaptations, emissions test
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective fix; may recur if engine has oil consumption

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: EGR valve + intake manifold clean, throttle body service, full adaptation reset
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses related carbon buildup; suitable for moderate-mileage Spade

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with EGR Delete Option
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt unit; EGR blanking plate fitted if MOT-compliant for your variant
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution for chronic EGR issues; confirm legality with specialist

problems.recommendation: EGR cleaning is worthwhile for Spade under 80k miles. For higher-mileage units or those with oil consumption, carbon recurrence is likely. Discuss EGR blanking options with your specialist to balance performance, emissions compliance, and long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare EGR-resolved Toyota Spade engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Toyota Spade
finalCta.paragraph: From VVT-i solenoid faults to hybrid inverter concerns, the Toyota Spade's most common engine problems have proven, cost-effective solutions. EngineMarket connects you with UK grey import specialists who understand JDM engine codes, CVT calibration, and ULEZ compliance requirements. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices from vetted suppliers today and keep your Spade on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Toyota Spade Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Toyota Spade and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Toyota Spade Petrol Engines

items.descriptor: The 1.5L 1NZ-FE petrol engine powered all non-hybrid Toyota Spade grey imports to the UK, valued for simplicity, Vitz/Porte parts sharing and widespread specialist familiarity.
items.familiesLabel: Common Toyota Spade Petrol Engine Families
families: 1NZ-FE - dominant replacement engine (Spade F/L/S), VVT-i solenoid and oil consumption concerns
2NZ-FE - early NCP130 applications, similar failure patterns to 1NZ-FE
1ZR-FE - late-model Spade S variants, improved oil control but CVT calibration critical
knownFor: Strong parts interchangeability with Toyota Vitz and Porte platforms
VVT-i solenoid failures between 60k-90k miles triggering P0011 codes
Oil consumption on units over 100k miles requiring piston ring upgrades
CVT compatibility critical - TCU adaptation reset essential post-install
items.cta: Compare Toyota Spade Petrol engine prices

items.title: Toyota Spade Hybrid Engines
items.description: The 1.5L 1NZ-FXE hybrid powertrain brought ULEZ compliance to the Toyota Spade from 2016, combining proven petrol reliability with electric assist for urban grey import owners.
items.descriptor: The 1.5L 1NZ-FXE hybrid powertrain brought ULEZ compliance to the Toyota Spade from 2016, combining proven petrol reliability with electric assist for urban grey import owners.
families: 1NZ-FXE - primary hybrid code (Spade Hybrid, ULEZ variants), inverter calibration critical
2ZR-FXE - late-model hybrid applications, similar hybrid system architecture
Petrol engine and electric motors work via planetary gearset for seamless power delivery
Regenerative braking recharges hybrid battery during deceleration and coasting
Engine replacement requires HV system isolation, inverter calibration and MG sync validation
foundIn: Spade Hybrid, Spade Hybrid CVT, Spade ULEZ Hybrid (NCP130 LCI, 2016-2020)
importantNotes: No plug-in hybrid version of the Toyota Spade exists in UK grey import market
Hybrid engine replacement requires specialist HV-certified technician and equipment
Availability more limited than petrol units; pre-order recommended for urgent replacements

engineYears.h2: Toyota Spade Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Toyota Spade NCP130 generation (2012-2020), organised so UK grey import owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The Spade launched with the proven 1NZ-FE 1.5L VVT-i petrol engine, establishing the platform's reputation for simplicity and Vitz/Porte parts sharing.
keyChanges: Toyota Spade NCP130 platform introduced to Japanese market
1NZ-FE 1.5L petrol engine standard across F, L, S trims
Super CVT-i automatic transmission option added for smoother urban driving
Sliding-door accessibility design established as core Spade selling point
knownFor: Strong initial demand from UK grey import specialists for accessibility-focused buyers
Early VVT-i solenoid concerns emerging on higher-mileage import units
CVT calibration critical for smooth installation in UK right-hand drive conversions
Parts interchangeability with Vitz/Porte simplifies sourcing for UK specialists
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2014 Toyota Spade replacement engines
years.ticker: Toyota Spade 1NZ-FE engine replacement • Spade VVT-i solenoid fix • Spade CVT compatibility check

years.preview: NCP130 Facelift: CVT Refinements & Hybrid Tease
years.description: Mid-life updates refined the CVT calibration and introduced early hybrid testing, setting the stage for the 2016 ULEZ-driven hybrid launch.
keyChanges: Updated Super CVT-i calibration for smoother low-speed operation
Minor interior trim revisions across F, L, S variants
Hybrid powertrain testing in Japanese domestic market ahead of UK grey import demand
Enhanced sliding-door motor control for improved accessibility reliability
knownFor: CVT shudder concerns on early units resolved via TCU adaptation updates
Oil consumption reports increasing on Spade units exceeding 80k miles
Growing UK demand for ULEZ-compliant options driving hybrid variant interest
Specialist garages developing Spade-specific fitting protocols for grey imports

faq.h2: Toyota Spade Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Toyota Spade engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Toyota Spade engine typically costs £800-£2,200 for petrol 1NZ-FE units and £1,200-£3,500 for hybrid 1NZ-FXE variants, supply-only. Supply & fit adds £400-£900 depending on location and variant complexity.
keyPoints: Petrol 1NZ-FE rebuilt engines start around £800 supply-only
Hybrid 1NZ-FXE units cost more due to inverter calibration requirements
CVT-compatible engines require TCU adaptation reset included in price
Supply & fit service adds labour, calibration and MOT-ready certification
items.cta: Compare Toyota Spade engine replacement prices today

items.question: What engine code does my Toyota Spade have?
items.answer: Most UK grey import Toyota Spade models use the 1NZ-FE 1.5L petrol engine code; hybrid variants from 2016 onwards use 1NZ-FXE. Check your cam cover stamp or VIN decoder for confirmation.
keyPoints: 1NZ-FE powers non-hybrid Spade F, L, S variants (2012-2020)
1NZ-FXE is the hybrid code for ULEZ-compliant Spade models (2016-2020)
CVT variants use Super CVT-i (petrol) or e-CVT (hybrid) transmission codes
Enter your registration above for instant DVLA-verified engine code lookup
items.cta: Get your Toyota Spade engine code verified instantly

items.question: Will a Toyota Vitz or Porte engine fit my Toyota Spade?
items.answer: Yes, the Toyota Spade shares the NCP130 platform with the Vitz and Porte, so 1NZ-FE and 1NZ-FXE engines are generally interchangeable with minor ECU coding adjustments for trim-specific features.
keyPoints: NCP130 platform ensures core engine mounting compatibility across models
ECU coding may be required for sliding-door or trim-specific electronics
CVT calibration essential regardless of donor vehicle model
Confirm engine code match before ordering to avoid fitment delays
items.cta: Check Vitz/Porte engine compatibility for your Spade

items.question: How long does a Toyota Spade engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only rebuilt engines typically dispatch in 3-5 working days; supply & fit services require pre-booking and usually complete within 1-2 days once the engine arrives at your chosen garage.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines bench-tested before dispatch to minimise post-fit issues
CVT/hybrid variants require additional calibration time post-installation
Pre-book fitting slots to avoid garage scheduling delays
MOT-ready certification included with supply & fit services
items.cta: Book your Toyota Spade engine supply & fit slot today

items.question: What warranty comes with a Toyota Spade recon engine?
items.answer: All rebuilt and reconditioned Toyota Spade engines supplied through EngineMarket include a standard 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists, covering parts and labour for manufacturing defects.
keyPoints: 12-month warranty standard across vetted UK supplier network
Unlimited mileage coverage ideal for taxi and high-use Spade owners
Warranty valid UK-wide; confirm transferability if selling the vehicle
Keep installation receipts and service records to maintain coverage
items.cta: View warranty terms for Toyota Spade engine replacements

items.question: Is a used Toyota Spade engine a good idea?
items.answer: Used JDM Toyota Spade engines offer budget replacement but carry higher risk; request compression test results and mileage verification before purchase to avoid premature failure.
keyPoints: Used engines typically cost £600-£1,600 but lack warranty depth
Always verify compression test and leak-down results before ordering
Budget for ancillary replacements: mounts, sensors, fluids, CVT adaptation
Rebuilt engines offer better long-term value with upgraded failure-point components
items.cta: Compare used vs rebuilt Toyota Spade engine options

items.question: Is replacing a Toyota Spade hybrid engine worth it?
items.answer: Yes, for ULEZ-compliant urban use, replacing a Spade hybrid engine preserves zero-charge CAZ access and exceptional fuel economy; the £1,200-£3,500 investment often exceeds the vehicle's remaining value but delivers regulatory compliance.
keyPoints: Hybrid Spade maintains ULEZ exemption worth £12.50/day in London
1NZ-FXE rebuilt units include inverter calibration and MG sync validation
Hybrid battery health check recommended during engine replacement
Specialist HV-certified installation essential for warranty validity
items.cta: Enquire about Toyota Spade hybrid engine replacement value

items.question: Do I need ECU coding after a Toyota Spade engine replacement?
items.answer: Yes, most Toyota Spade engine replacements require ECU coding for immobiliser reset, CVT adaptation, and trim-specific feature sync; ensure your specialist includes this in the supply & fit quote.
keyPoints: Immobiliser reset essential for engine start post-installation
CVT/e-CVT adaptation prevents drivability issues and warning lights
Sliding-door module sync may be required for accessibility variants
Confirm coding inclusion before booking to avoid post-fit diagnostics costs
items.cta: Find Toyota Spade specialists offering ECU coding included

items.question: What oil should I use in a rebuilt Toyota Spade engine?
items.answer: Use Toyota-approved 0W-20 or 5W-30 fully synthetic oil meeting API SN/ILSAC GF-5 specifications for rebuilt 1NZ-FE/1NZ-FXE engines; follow break-in procedures for first 500 miles.
keyPoints: 0W-20 preferred for hybrid variants to optimise fuel economy
High-mileage formulations acceptable for units over 100k miles
Change oil and filter at 500 miles post-install for break-in protection
Maintain regular 6-month/6k-mile service intervals thereafter
items.cta: Get Toyota Spade engine oil specification guidance

items.question: How do I know if my Toyota Spade needs an engine replacement?
items.answer: Major warning signs include persistent blue smoke (oil burning), metallic knocking (bearing failure), coolant loss with white exhaust (head gasket), or hybrid system warnings that persist after basic diagnostics.
keyPoints: Compression test below 120 PSI on any cylinder indicates replacement need
Persistent P0011/P0012 codes after solenoid replacement suggest internal timing wear
Hybrid warning lights with reduced power often indicate inverter or MG faults
Get a free diagnostic assessment before committing to replacement
items.cta: Get a free Toyota Spade engine diagnostic assessment
